title: Cratered wall inside Nerv HQ
wikitext:
[]: Cratered wall inside Nerv HQ?             Quote Post by subAtomic » Wed Jun 30, 2010 12:52 am Hi, I've been reading these great threads for the last few days, and haven't seen anybody mention this, so I thought I'd join. Around 8 min 40 sec into Rebuild 1.11, Misato and Shinji-kun are riding the moving walkway, approaching door R-08. As the door slides open, there is a VERY brief glimpse of where they are headed, which is toward a massive purplish-black wall absolutely covered in impact craters. It looks like the surface of the moon. I don't remember seeing this in the original series, but it's been 10 years since I watched it. Anyone with more NGE knowledge than myself care to elaborate? Thanks!

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Wed Jun 30, 2010 3:22 am I caught the frame you were referring to (too lazy to make a screencap). The simple explanation: since they are in an underground tunnel, the craters are small asymmetries within the bedrock in b4 Flinstones that the tunnel was dug through.

[]:             Quote Post by subAtomic » Wed Jun 30, 2010 4:05 am Thanks for the reply, SaltyJoe. See the lightened screenshot below. Those are craters you only pick up in outer space.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Jun 30, 2010 4:08 am Could be a subtle clue that the geofront originated from space.

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Wed Jun 30, 2010 4:09 am It's the Black Moon, isn't it? They're deep in the GeoFront. This shot was probably inserted into 1.11 to, among other things, show that the Black Moon is still under Tokyo-3.

[]:             Quote Post by subAtomic » Wed Jun 30, 2010 4:17 am I guess that's what I was getting at, MugwumpHasNoLiver (great username!) That is clearly the cratered surface of Black Moon. The thing is, they're already deep within GeoFront, aren't they? And GeoFront is Black Moon. So either a) Black Moon is housed within/below, and no longer synonymous with, the GeoFront in Rebuild or b) Misato is so turned around she's actually lead them back out of GeoFront and now they're re-entering it. So it's either a clue to a huge revision in the story or a subtle joke. :) Either way, it's a beautiful, but brief, glimpse of the Black Moon's ancient and extraterrestrial origin.

[]:             Quote Post by DarthGendo » Wed Jun 30, 2010 4:32 am It's certainly looks like a Black Moon. But it is 11 km in diameter or so. It would be more logical if they would descend down into it. Probably a bug from creators' side.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Wed Jun 30, 2010 7:33 am subAtomic wrote: The thing is, they're already deep within GeoFront, aren't they?

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Wed Jun 30, 2010 9:26 am I know there was a thread about this, but I can't find it for whatever reason. Sorry I can't be more helpful.

[]:             Quote Post by Dark doom » Wed Jun 30, 2010 10:40 am It could be a vivi-sected part of the Black moon. That would account for it's smaller size.

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Wed Jun 30, 2010 11:39 am It's a typo.

[]:             Quote Post by SpacemanSchmuck » Wed Jun 30, 2010 11:56 am Black Moon, agreed. A really nice touch by the filmmakers, and a great catch, subAtomic.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jun 30, 2010 12:14 pm I'm assuming it is part of the Black Moon, personally, but who knows. It's a very awesome touch either way and you guys have a good eye. I didn't even notice that shot when watching 1.11!

[]:             Quote Post by subAtomic » Wed Jun 30, 2010 1:17 pm I can't believe it's a goof of the production team. They seem to really know what they're doing here. I have confidence that this is not a "creators' bug". I also tend to doubt that it is a vivisectioned piece of the Black Moon. Removing a section of the shell would weaken it, like any egg. I was going to phrase this as a what if? but it became one long, rambling question, so I'm posing it as a wild theory instead. I'm not endorsing it, but I'm curious if anyone can disprove it or explain logically why it can't/shouldn't be so: The GeoFront in Rebuild is not the same thing as the Black Moon. The GeoFront is a natural or man-made cavern beneath Tokyo-3, and the Black Moon (smaller than it was in the original NGE), is buried deep beneath it. Instead of housing the entire GeoFront, the Black Moon in Rebuild is where the most sensitive and secret technology and information is stored.

[]:             Quote Post by DarthGendo » Wed Jun 30, 2010 9:44 pm My 5 cents. Scene, where Misa & Shin go down into Terminal Dogma. More insight on Black Moon's internal structure. Maybe the name "Lilith's Egg" is more literal this time.
